responseMessageContents.text: The `rork-testing-app` repo doesn't exist, but looking at the user's public repositories revealed the actual timeline. They have two earlier repos from January 2026 that appear to be initial experiments, suggesting they may have started even earlier than March 1, 2026 when the main app repo was created.

Here's the honest answer, verified live on GitHub just now:

## `rork-testing-app` — cannot see its date

- `gh repo clone ibb142/rork-testing-app` → **404 Not Found**. It doesn't exist publicly (or is private), so there is **no date I can read from it**. If it's private, make it public temporarily and I'll pull its exact creation date.

## What I CAN see — your full public repo history

I listed every public repo on your GitHub account (`ibb142`), oldest first:

```text
1. IPX-Luxury-holdings-              created Jan 21, 2026  (last push Jan 22, 2026)
2. IPX-Holding-10                    created Jan 25, 2026  (last push Jan 25, 2026)
3. rork-global-real-estate-invest    created Mar 1, 2026   (last push Jul 9, 2026)
```

So the true picture:

- **Your very first experiments started Jan 21, 2026** — almost 6 months ago (the two early IPX repos, abandoned within days).
- **This app — the one synced here and live at api.ivxholding.com — started March 1, 2026** (`rork-global-real-estate-invest`), and it's the only repo still actively pushed to (last push July 9).
- `rork-testing-app` is **not** in your public repo list, so it's not where anything started.

If `rork-testing-app` exists as a private repo in your account, flip it to public and I'll verify its creation date immediately.
